Yet, shortly after when restrictions began to be lifted, The Help pushed me to approach school differently. Not only did it influence me to create a community, it also taught me that hiding behind the next thing in front of you can hinder success. After this realization, I no longer limited myself to what I found familiar but instead embraced the unlimited possibilities that lie within the people I have yet to meet while building a supportive community to help you thrive not only in the transition between schools, but also the future journeys that have yet to be experienced. No matter the challenges Aibileen and Samantha faced, they still persevered to chase their dreams of becoming better not only for themselves but for those suffering around them as well. During the transition, this instilled in me that discomfort can lead to growth. The Help’s emphasis on chasing after dreams, whether it be my future in medicine or even just getting into my dream school, taught me that the path to such passions may seem rocky and restless but can be worth it in the end.
